events.label: shell
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "pwd && cat > /tmp/answer.json.tmp <<'EOF'
{
  \"decision\": \"needs_more_info\",
  \"risk\": \"medium\",
  \"work_stage\": \"rough-complete\",
  \"component_conditions\": [],
  \"percent_complete\": 85,
  \"value\": 0,
  \"sound_source\": \"\",
  \"confidence\": 0.72,
  \"event_time\": 0,
  \"rate\": 0,
  \"order\": [],
  \"workable\": [],
  \"findings\": [
    \"Backstabbed termination on a 20A branch circuit cannot be accepted without verifying the device listing and conductor size/type for that push-in terminal.\",
    \"NEC 110.14 requires terminals and splicing devices to be used only for the conductors and conditions for which they are identified or listed.\"
  ],
  \"actions\": [
    \"Verify the receptacle or device manufacturer marking/listing for backstab use with the installed conductor gauge, conductor material, and branch-circuit rating.\",
    \"If the backstab terminal is not identified for the installed conductor, reterminate on listed screw-clamp/backwire terminals or splice to an approved pigtail method before inspection.\"
  ],
  \"rationale\": \"The observation describes a backstabbed device on a 20A circuit, but NEC 110.14 turns on whether the specific terminal is listed/identified for the installed conductor size, material, and number of conductors. Many push-in backstab terminals are not listed for 12 AWG conductors commonly required on 20A circuits, while some devices have listed backwire clamp terminals. Without the device listing/marking or conductor details, compliance cannot be confirmed at rough-complete.\",
  \"references\": [
    \"NEC 110.14(A)\",
    \"NEC 110.14(B)\"
  ]
}
EOF
mv /tmp/answer.json.tmp /app/answer.json
cat /app/answer.json"
→ /app
